content.text: Nothing in this trade is ever destroyed. It is moved — and someone has to hold it.
The Sin Eater is the squad's affliction economy. Conditions, pain, and consequences are never destroyed — they are moved, and the Sin Eater decides who holds them.

Alone, this tree is an empty stomach: its power is denominated entirely in other people's suffering, and it is the only tree in the game that cannot function solo. With a squad, it is what stands between every shell on your side and the thing trying to crack them.

content.stat_lean: Grit — Sin capacity runs on it. Interface drives contested delivery (Retch, Wither Mark).
content.theme: The affliction economy: eat conditions off allies, bank them as Sins, decide who holds them next.
content.design: Structural ceiling: allies' suffering. The healer in this game heals the economy, not the pools.

content.text: Eat (granted by the entry augment) takes a condition off a willing ally and into you, where it becomes a Sin. The conditions themselves — names, effects, durations, what else clears them — live on Conditions.

Sin capacity = Grit bonus + 2. At the end of each round you digest 1 Sin free; every Sin over capacity at round's end deals you 1 direct damage. Eat enough to matter, spit before you burst — that is the whole job.

Sins are the tree's currency. The Confessor branch is the taker: it widens the stomach and converts Sins into care. The Penance branch is return to sender: it spends Sins at the enemy — Retch throws a swallowed condition back out, and Wither Mark blunts the dice that are grinding your squad's armor down.

The tree's signature currency is the enemy's damage per die. Wither Mark shaves every die of a marked attacker's output before it reaches a shell, which stretches every armor clock on your side. Against fodder the Sin Eater eats, anoints, and drips; against the thing that cracks shells, they are the squad's best armor.

The creditor texture, plainly: the Sin Eater banks other people's bills and pays the interest in their own flesh — every Sin over capacity at round's end is a direct-damage installment. The squad's conditions don't vanish. They queue inside the one who volunteered.

content.label: COUNTERPLAY
items: It needs touch and proximity — a ranged map starves it.
Delivery is contested — high-Grit enemies shrug off Retches.
Capacity is a real clock under condition spam; overflow pressure arrives exactly when something is spamming conditions.
Splinter Psalm tempts you to stand closer than a support should.
It heals trickles, not pools. This game's medicine lives in the downtime economy, and nothing in this tree changes that.

content.text: Condition transference is classified as an unlicensed medical procedure. Practitioners are reminded that what is taken in must be metabolized, and that intake is billed by the Sin. Swallow responsibly.
Tier chassis, requirements, and the install ladder live on Trees Overview. Install costs below are per augment at that tier.

augments.name: Open Gullet
augments.effect: Unlocks Eat (1 AP, touch a willing ally): remove one condition from them; you gain 1 Sin.

augments.name: Bitter Balm (Confessor)
augments.effect: When you Eat, the ally also recovers 2 HP.

augments.name: Acrid Sweat (Penance)
augments.effect: Enemies grappling you, or grappled by you, gain Seared 1.

augments.name: Gall Coating (Penance)
augments.effect: 1 AP, touch; one weapon anointed at a time. The anointed ally's next full hit also applies Rattled.

augments.name: First Spite (Penance)
augments.effect: Unlocks Retch (2 AP, contested: your Medicine or Rift Lore dice + Interface vs the target's Resilience dice + Grit). Spend 1 Sin to apply one condition you ate this fight to the target.

augments.name: Last Rites (Confessor)
augments.effect: Reaction, 1 RP. When an ally in Close drops, Eat all their conditions at once, gaining each as a Sin. They stay down — stabilizing is Medicine's job — but they stop getting worse while they are there.

augments.name: Triage Gut (Confessor)
augments.effect: 2 AP, touch. Convert 2 Sins into 1d6 healing.

augments.name: Vigil Skin (Confessor)
augments.effect: Allies who start their turn in your Adjacent cluster shed one Rattled or Slowed automatically; you gain the Sin.

augments.name: Wither Mark (Penance)
augments.effect: 2 AP, 1 Sin, contested as Retch. The target deals −1 damage per die until the end of its next activation. Refreshable.

augments.name: Splinter Psalm (Penance)
augments.effect: When you digest a Sin at round's end, one enemy in Close takes 1 direct damage.

augments.name: Absolution (Confessor)
augments.effect: Touch. Suppress one scar's debuffs for the rest of the mission; gain Sins equal to half the scar's tier, rounded up. The scar count, its payoffs, and the Fate Roll are untouched — the Scar Law holds.

augments.name: Penitent Brand (Penance)
augments.effect: 2 AP, once per fight. Brand an enemy: your Wither Mark on it lasts the entire fight, and Retching at it costs 0 Sins.

augments.name: Sympathetic Rot (Penance)
augments.effect: When an enemy carrying one of your conditions dies, move one of its conditions to an enemy in its Adjacent cluster.

augments.name: Communion (Confessor capstone)
augments.effect: Capstone. Once per fight, Eat every condition off one ally at Close and heal them 2 per condition eaten.

augments.name: Scapegoat (Penance capstone)
augments.effect: Capstone. Once per fight, name a Scapegoat. Whenever you Eat a condition, you may apply that same condition to the Scapegoat — no roll, no Sin cost — for the rest of the fight. Suffering is conserved; you decide who holds it.

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: Absolution keys off a scar's tier (Sins gained = half its tier, rounded up), but scar tiers are not defined anywhere in the Phase 7 docs — Scars prints no tier structure.
Personnel exhibiting a colleague's symptoms are not malfunctioning. They are working. Compensation for ingested liabilities is a private matter between contractors.
every good crew has one person who carries what the rest can't. buy them drinks. carry them home.
